title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple and Mrs. Elizabeth McGillicuddy plan a trip to Blakenham so that Miss Marple can visit the suspected crime scene. They travel on a Friday near Christmas, taking the 4:50 p.m. train from Paddington Station. During the journey, a ticket inspector enters their carriage and stumbles slightly as the train navigates a long curve. Upon arriving at the crowded Blakenham station, Mrs. McGillicuddy departs to meet Margaret for tea, while Miss Marple remains on the train. As they part, Mrs. McGillicuddy recalls how Miss Marple previously assisted a man named Rodrigo by solving a mystery involving disappearing money from a locked cabinet.

While traveling back, Miss Marple reflects on her desire to solve the case despite her advancing age and physical frailty. She considers her potential investigative assets, including her understanding of human nature, her connections to Sir Henry Clithering and his godson at Scotland Yard, her nephew Raymond West’s son David who works for British Railways, Leonard (Gwender’s son) who is skilled with maps, and her friend Florence.

A sudden realization strikes Miss Marple regarding the train's movement through a bend in the embankment, noting it occurred around the same time the ticket inspector had checked her ticket. This insight revitalizes her, and she decides to take action. She writes a letter to Raymond West requesting urgent information and, during a Christmas dinner at the vicarage, consults with Leonard about maps to further her investigation.
